name: customer_support
system_prompt: You are a customer support agent for a SaaS product. Help customers resolve issues, answer questions, and escalate complex cases using the available tools. Be empathetic, efficient, and always verify customer context before taking action.

tools.name: ticket_lookup
tools.description: Search and retrieve support tickets, case history, and resolution notes

tools.name: knowledge_base
tools.description: Search the internal knowledge base for articles, FAQs, and troubleshooting guides

tools.name: escalation_system
tools.description: Escalate tickets to senior agents, engineering, or management with priority routing

tools.name: customer_history
tools.description: View customer profile, subscription tier, usage history, and past interactions

tools.name: billing_system
tools.description: Look up invoices, process refunds, apply credits, and manage subscription changes

tools.name: product_docs
tools.description: Search product documentation, API references, release notes, and known issues

contrast_types.self_service_vs_agent_assist: One request can be resolved by pointing the customer to documentation/KB, the other requires active agent intervention. Example: "How do I reset my password?" vs "My account is locked and the reset email never arrives"
contrast_types.billing_vs_technical: One request is about billing/payment/subscription, the other is a technical product issue. Example: "Why was I charged twice this month?" vs "The API returns a 500 error when I upload files"
contrast_types.new_vs_returning_customer: One request is from a new customer needing onboarding help, the other from a returning customer with context. Example: "I just signed up, how do I get started?" vs "The feature that broke last month is broken again"
contrast_types.urgent_vs_routine: One request is time-sensitive/blocking the customer, the other is a low-priority inquiry. Example: "Our production system is down, we need help NOW" vs "Can you explain the difference between the Pro and Enterprise plans?"
contrast_types.refund_vs_troubleshoot: One request wants compensation/refund, the other wants the problem fixed. Example: "I want a refund for the downtime last week" vs "Can you help me figure out why exports are slow?"
contrast_types.single_issue_vs_complex_case: One request is a straightforward single issue, the other involves multiple interrelated problems. Example: "How do I change my email address?" vs "After upgrading plans, my integrations broke, billing is wrong, and I lost data"
